metadata.summary: A 5-phase sequential implementation roadmap for operationalizing a PLG model: Foundation, Free Tier Design, Activation Optimization, Monetization, and Expansion.

content.title: PLG Implementation Guide
content.summary: A 5-phase sequential implementation roadmap for operationalizing a PLG model: (1) Foundation — define Aha moment and measure TTV; (2) Free Tier Design — limits, upgrade triggers, self-serve checkout; (3) Activation Optimization — onboarding, templates, TTV under 5 minutes; (4) Monetization — PQL scoring, upgrade prompts, automated playbooks; (5) Expansion — seat/usage triggers, expansion revenue tracking. Run phases in order — each depends on prior instrumentation.

why.p: PLG strategy without an implementation plan produces incomplete funnels: Aha moment defined but not instrumented, upgrade triggers built without PQL scoring, expansion playbooks running before activation is optimized. This guide maps the strategy (plg-basics) and tactics (plg-optimization-tactics) to engineering tickets across 5 sequential phases with concrete playbooks for freemium-to-paid, trial-to-paid, and expansion.

list.item: Operationalizing a PLG model after plg-basics selected one.
Producing a phased rollout plan that maps to engineering tickets.
Running freemium-to-paid, trial-to-paid, or expansion playbooks as deterministic Day-N sequences.
Selecting and justifying the PLG tech stack (analytics, onboarding, billing, PQL scoring).
High-level PLG model choice — route to plg-basics.
Stage-specific tactic catalog — route to plg-optimization-tactics.
Metric definitions and PQL math — route to plg-metrics.
Pre-PMF teams without an Aha-moment hypothesis — running this checklist before PMF wastes engineering cycles.

section.name: Aha Moment Discovery, TTV Optimization, and 5-Phase Checklist

rule.statement: Find the Aha moment using two methods: (1) behavioral correlation — find the action most correlated with Day-30 retention in converted vs churned cohorts, minimum 200 converted users; (2) qualitative validation — interview 5+ converted customers asking "when did you decide to pay?". Both must agree before treating the Aha event as validated.
rule.rationale: Behavioral correlation alone overfits to noisy events. Qualitative interviews confirm causality vs coincidence. Requiring both methods reduces false Aha moment choices by ~60% vs cohort-only approaches.

list.item: Slack Aha: first message sent in a channel with teammates.
Dropbox Aha: first file synced across two devices.
Zoom Aha: first successful video call completed.
Notion Aha: first page created and shared with a collaborator.

rule.statement: Target TTV (time from signup to Aha moment) under 5 minutes. This requires: pre-configured demo state or sample data, social login option, skip option for all non-critical steps, and a single guided path to the core action.
rule.rationale: TTV under 5 minutes is achievable only with templates or pre-seeded data — an empty product with a setup wizard is rarely under 15 minutes. Pre-seeding is an engineering requirement, not a UX recommendation.

list.item: Progressive onboarding (learn while doing): high impact.
Pre-configured templates and example data: high impact.
Interactive tutorial with guided first action: high impact.
Social login (Google, GitHub): medium impact, removes password friction.
Single-player mode (defer team features until solo value proven): medium impact.

section.name: 5-Phase Implementation Checklist
section.p: Phase 1 — Foundation: define Aha moment, measure current TTV, map user journey to value, identify conversion triggers, set up product analytics.
Phase 2 — Free Tier Design: choose PLG model, define free tier limits, create upgrade triggers, design self-serve checkout, build pricing page.
Phase 3 — Activation Optimization: simplify signup to under 3 fields, create onboarding flow, add templates/examples, build interactive tutorial, reduce TTV to under 5 minutes.
Phase 4 — Monetization: define PQL criteria, build PQL scoring (see plg-metrics), create upgrade prompts, set up automated email playbooks, add sales-assist for ACV above $1K/month.
Phase 5 — Expansion: identify expansion triggers (seat growth, usage growth), build seat/usage upgrade flows, create upsell playbooks, track expansion revenue and net revenue retention.

rule.statement: Show upgrade triggers contextually at the moment of need: at 80% of limit consumption, when user attempts a gated feature, when team collaboration signal fires. Never show during first session, during a critical workflow, or more than once per session.
rule.rationale: Contextual triggers convert at 3-5x time-based pop-ups. First-session prompts break trust before value is demonstrated.

rule.statement: All upgrade trigger copy must reference the user's actual behavior ("You've created 4 of 5 projects") not generic upsell language ("Get more features").
rule.rationale: Behavior-personalized copy converts at 2-3x generic copy in PLG upgrade prompt tests.

rule.statement: Trigger the freemium-to-paid playbook at 80% of limit consumption (not 100%). All messages must queue with approved=False and require human review before send.
rule.rationale: Triggering at 100% means the user has already hit a wall; at 80% the message feels helpful rather than punitive. Auto-sending campaigns without human review risks mis-sent mass communications.

list.item: Day 0: in-app limit notification — "You have 1 project remaining. Pro plan unlocks unlimited."
Day 2: email if no action — value of upgrading + social proof from a similar company.
Day 5: in-app reminder — remaining capacity with specific upgrade benefit.
Day 7: email — time-limited discount offer (20% off first 3 months).
Trial Days 1-3 (Activation focus): welcome email with quickstart, in-app checklist toward Aha moment, chat widget for questions.
Trial Days 4-7 (Value demonstration): usage-report email citing what the user accomplished, feature discovery prompts, social proof from similar companies.
Trial Days 8-11 (Conversion intent): PQL scoring check; high PQL score triggers sales outreach; low PQL stays in automated nurture.
Trial Days 12-14 (Urgency): countdown in product UI, final email with actual usage stats injected ("You've created X projects"), clear upgrade CTA.

rule.statement: Trigger expansion playbook from leading signals — 3+ active users from same org, @mentions in comments, external sharing — not from admin seat count alone. Route high-PQL accounts above ACV $1K/month to sales SDR; below that, automation closes the loop.
rule.rationale: Leading behavioral signals predict expansion intent days before seat limits are reached. Waiting for the seat limit to fire gives competitors time to make the case for a switch at renewal.

list.item: In-product message to any user: "Your team is growing — add more members on Team plan."
Email to admin: team activity report showing collaboration value, Team plan benefits.
High PQL score: sales call offer for Team or Enterprise tier.

rule.statement: Ship Phase 1 (Foundation) end-to-end before starting Phase 2. The checklist order is sequential because each phase depends on prior instrumentation.
rule.rationale: Building upgrade triggers (Phase 2) before measuring TTV (Phase 1) optimizes a funnel with no baseline; lift cannot be measured without before/after instrumentation.

rule.statement: Self-serve checkout instrumentation must include payment-failure events. Tracking only successful upgrades misses the largest BoF leak in most PLG products.
rule.rationale: Payment failures are the most common bottom-of-funnel drop-off and are often 2-3x more frequent than successful upgrades. Invisible failures prevent any checkout optimization.

list.item: The 5-phase checklist is a maturity ladder, not a roadmap; agents should not promise sequential delivery without a parallel-tracks variant.
Aha-moment discovery requires sufficient converted-cohort data (≥ ~200 paid users); below that, statistical correlation is unreliable and human judgment dominates.
Day-N playbook timings (Day 2/5/7/14) are heuristics from B2B SaaS — B2C, dev tools, and creator-economy products have different windows.
"Tools & Tech Stack" table mixes free + enterprise tiers; agents must include cost + integration-effort columns before recommending.
PQL section defers to plg-metrics — agents must chain reads, not invent scoring locally.

section.name: Agentic Workflow
section.p: This methodology maps directly to a multi-agent state machine: an aha-moment-finder mines retained-vs-churned cohorts, a ttv-instrumenter adds the funnel events from signup to Aha, a playbook-runner drives the freemium / trial / expansion playbooks as scheduled jobs (Day 0/2/5/7 etc.), and a phase-tracker walks the 5-phase implementation checklist as Linear/Jira issues. Human approval gates: free-tier limit numbers, pricing-page changes, sales-assist handoffs.

Recommended subagents: aha-moment-finder (pulls cohort behavior data, returns the action most correlated with retention + confidence interval). ttv-instrumenter (emits a funnel-event spec and a PR plan to add them). playbook-runner (schedules the freemium/trial/expansion sequences in Customer.io / Klaviyo / Intercom; renders templates + waits). pql-scorer (wraps the PQL definition from plg-metrics and assigns scores nightly). phase-tracker (converts the 5-phase implementation checklist into trackable issues, reports completion %).

list.item: mixpanel JQL — Aha-moment correlation analysis on cohorts
amplitude Behavioral Cohorts API — PQL behavioral cohort sync
posthog cohort + flag API — Run free/trial/paid cohorts and feature flags
customerio API — Drive Day-N playbook campaigns programmatically
klaviyo API — Trial-to-paid email sequences with metric triggers
intercom API — In-app messaging + chat for trial assistance
madkudu / breeze (HubSpot) — PQL scoring services
stripe Billing API — Plan changes, proration, downgrade flows
Amplitude / Mixpanel / PostHog — Aha-moment + TTV instrumentation
Appcues / Pendo / Userflow / Chameleon — Onboarding flows, checklists, in-app upgrade triggers
Intercom / Drift / Crisp — Chat assistance for trial-day-1 pattern
Hotjar / FullStory / Heap — Diagnose where users miss Aha
Stripe / Paddle / Chargebee — Self-serve checkout
Customer.io / Autopilot / Klaviyo — Day-N email automation
Madkudu / Clearbit Reveal — PQL scoring + lead enrichment
Ship Phase 1 (Foundation) end-to-end before starting Phase 2 — the order is sequential because each phase depends on the previous instrumentation.
For Aha-moment work, validate with at least two methods: behavioral correlation + qualitative converted-user interviews.
TTV target less than 5 minutes is aggressive but achievable only with templates/sample data — bake the seeding into onboarding or the metric is theatre.
Day-N playbook content must reference user behavior ("you've created 4 of 5 projects"), not generic upsell copy.
PQL handoff to sales triggers ONLY above the model-defined ACV cutoff; below that, automation closes the loop.
For expansion playbook, watch leading signals (3+ active users, @mentions, external sharing) — not just paid-seat counts.
Always pair upgrade-trigger UI changes with measurement: track impression, click, dismiss, post-click conversion separately.
LLM-generated playbook copy drifts to "Hi there, hope you're doing well" filler. Force tone-anchor against existing converting messages.
Day-N playbook must NOT auto-send without human review; agents queue, humans approve. Even with confidence guardrails, mis-sent campaigns are brand-damaging.
Aha-moment discovery is correlation, not causation — agents will overfit to noisy events. Require ≥30% lift vs random control + p less than 0.05.
Free-tier limit changes ripple to revenue forecasting; never let an agent change limits autonomously, even with phase-tracker green-light.
Self-serve checkout instrumentation must include payment-failure events; agents that skip these miss the largest BoF leak.
For trial-day-12 urgency emails, the agent should personalize with actual usage stats — generic "your trial ends" emails underperform; require usage-property injection.
Do NOT auto-trigger sales outreach from PQL signals without an SLA — bad timing kills conversion.

template.purpose: Python scheduler: generates freemium-to-paid Day-N message queue with approved=False flag for human review before send.
